category_title: Students with initial anxiety and echolalia-focused language
scenario_title: Intervention session with directive teacher approach
minimal_context: The teacher and student are in the school dinner hall with a dog, attempting to play throw and catch.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time for our activity. We are going to play throw and catch with the dog.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you want to do with the ball?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please stand up and throw the ball.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Shows little attention to the teacher, engages in playing alone and sensory/ritualistic behaviour)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for our activity. We are going to play throw and catch with the dog.

STUDENT: (Withdraws from the dog) Bear.

TUTOR: What do you want to do with the ball?

STUDENT: (Talks about favourite cartoons)

TUTOR: Please stand up and throw the ball.

STUDENT: (Shows little attention to the teacher, engages in playing alone and sensory/ritualistic behaviour)

task.content: 20-minute intervention sessions comparing time with teacher alone vs. time with dog present
task.learning_goal: Transitioning from echolalia/scripted speech to functional language and increasing interest and engagement

context.materials: Dog, rope for tug of war, balls for catch
context.social_setting: Time alone with the teacher or with the dog present

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the teacher uses a directive and questioning approach or imposes a prescheduled framework of activities
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Shows little attention or engagement with the teacher, engages in playing alone or sensory/ritualistic behaviour, and speech comprises mainly talk about favourite cartoons

behavior_patterns.condition: Initially when the dog is present
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Withdrew from interactions, referred to him as a 'bear'
behavior_patterns.internal_state: A little anxious of the dog
